disclaimer: 本檔由 PDF 自動萃取，levels 為 ACGME 英文原文；name_zh 由內建翻譯表提供，未匹配項目以英文呈現，可後續人工校對補上中譯。

levels.1: Elicits and reports the basic elements of an endocrine-specific history for common presentations Performs and reports the basic elements of an endocrine-specific physical exam for common presentations
levels.2: Elicits and concisely reports a thorough endocrine-specific history for common presentations Performs and concisely reports an endocrine-specific physical exam for common presentations
levels.3: Efficiently elicits and concisely reports an endocrine-specific history for complex presentations Performs and concisely reports an endocrine-specific physical exam for complex presentations
levels.4: Efficiently elicits and concisely reports an endocrine-specific history for rare endocrine presentations Performs and concisely reports an endocrine-specific exam for rare endocrine presentations
levels.5: Elicits and reports atypical history or physical exam findings which deviate from usual presentations of common, complex, and rare endocrine disorders

subcompetencies.name_en: Diagnostic Testing, including Labs, Imaging, and Dynamic Testing
subcompetencies.name_zh: Diagnostic Testing, including Labs, Imaging, and Dynamic Testing

levels.1: Orders basic tests for patients with common endocrine conditions Interprets basic endocrine test results
levels.2: Orders targeted tests for patients with common endocrine conditions using medical evidence Independently interprets targeted tests for common endocrine conditions
levels.3: Orders targeted tests for patients with complex endocrine conditions Interprets targeted tests for patients with complex endocrine conditions, with assistance, and identifies incongruencies
levels.4: Develops individualized cost-effective testing strategies to evaluate patients with complex endocrine conditions and avoids unnecessary testing Independently interprets targeted tests for patients with complex endocrine conditions and resolves incongruencies
levels.5: Identifies, critically evaluates, and selectively uses emerging and investigational tests or procedures; questions and reports unknown and unexplained discrepancies

levels.1: Prescribes basic endocrine therapeutics and describes their indications and adverse effects or reactions
levels.2: Prescribes and monitors therapeutics used in patients with common endocrine conditions, based on patient factors
levels.3: Prescribes and monitors the response to pharmacotherapy used in the management of patients with complex endocrine conditions
levels.4: Integrates the best available evidence to prescribe, monitor, and assess the response to pharmacotherapy used in the management of patients with common, complex, and rare endocrine conditions
levels.5: Identifies targeted or experimental therapies for complex and rare clinical scenarios

levels.3: Performs more complex procedures, with moderate supervision

levels.1: Respectfully receives consultation requests and responds with assistance Recognizes disease acuity, with supervision
levels.2: Clearly and concisely provides consultation Independently recognizes disease acuity
levels.3: Verifies understanding of recommendations with the primary team when providing consultation Recognizes disease acuity and prioritizes management steps
levels.4: Effectively conveys consultative assessment, rationale, and contingency plans to all health care team members Mobilizes resources to provide care in high-acuity situations
levels.5: Is identified as a role model for the provision of consultative care across the spectrum of disease complexity and acuity

levels.1: Identifies the need to request a consultation
levels.2: Clearly articulates the reason for requesting a consultation
levels.3: Checks one’s own understanding of recommendations when receiving consultation
levels.4: Coordinates recommendations from different consultants to optimize patient care
levels.5: Facilitates conflict resolution between and among consultants when disagreement exists

subcompetencies.name_en: Integration of Pathophysiology and Clinical Reasoning
subcompetencies.name_zh: Integration of Pathophysiology and Clinical Reasoning

levels.1: Demonstrates knowledge of pathophysiology of common endocrine conditions and develops a differential diagnosis
levels.2: Applies knowledge of pathophysiology to explain common presentations and prioritizes the differential diagnoses and therapeutic options
levels.3: Applies knowledge of pathophysiology to explain complex presentations and critically assesses diagnostic assumptions and therapeutic options
levels.4: Integrates advanced knowledge of pathophysiology to recognize and explain the clinical presentations of and therapeutic options for a rare condition
levels.5: Integrates knowledge of nuanced aspects of pathophysiology toward individualized diagnostic and therapeutic approaches, while challenging conventional ideas

levels.1: Identifies areas worthy of scholarly investigation, with supervision
levels.2: Designs a scholarly project, with supervision
levels.3: Implements a scholarly project, with supervision
levels.4: Produces scholarly work suitable for dissemination
levels.5: Publishes original scholarly work that has generated new medical knowledge, enduring educational materials, or process improvement

levels.1: Identifies and reports patient safety events using the institutional reporting system and discloses them to leadership, patients, and patients’ family members Demonstrates basic knowledge of quality improvement methodologies and metrics
levels.2: Analyzes the factors that contributed to a patient safety event Identifies opportunities for quality improvement projects
levels.3: Offers prevention strategies to mitigate patient safety events Participates in quality improvement projects
levels.4: Participates in efforts to modify systems to prevent patient safety events Demonstrates the skills required to identify, develop, implement, and analyze a quality improvement project
levels.5: Leads efforts to modify systems to prevent patient safety events Leads quality improvement projects

levels.1: Identifies the important elements of transitions and coordination of care
levels.2: Safely and effectively transitions and coordinates care of patients in routine clinical situations
levels.3: Effectively transitions and coordinates care of patients utilizing interprofessional teams in complex clinical situations
levels.4: Role models effective coordination and transition of care
levels.5: Leads in the design and implementation of improvements to the care coordination process

subcompetencies.name_en: Physician Role in Health Care Systems and Community Health
subcompetencies.name_zh: Physician Role in Health Care Systems and Community Health

levels.1: Identifies key components of the health care and payment systems; identifies health care needs in the community
levels.2: Describes how complex health care and payment systems impact patient care and contribute to health care barriers
levels.3: Advocates within the health care system and payor to provide cost-effective, patient-centered care and reduce barriers
levels.4: Advocates for adapting local practices to provide for the needs of specific populations and communities with health care needs
levels.5: Adapts local practices to provide for the needs of specific populations and communities with health care needs Advocates for health policy changes

levels.1: Articulates clinical questions to guide evidence-based care
levels.2: Locates and applies the best available evidence, integrated with patient values and preferences, to the care of patients with common conditions
levels.3: Locates and applies the best available evidence, integrated with patient values and preference, to the care of patients with complex and rare conditions
levels.4: Critically appraises conflicting or ambiguous evidence to guide individualized patient care
levels.5: Mentors others to critically appraise and apply evidence

subcompetencies.name_en: Reflective Practice and Commitment to Personal Growth
subcompetencies.name_zh: 反思實踐與個人成長承諾

levels.2: Modifies behavior based on feedback and establishes personal and professional goals based on gaps in knowledge and skills
levels.3: Seeks feedback; creates and implements an individualized learning plan
levels.4: Uses performance data to assess the effectiveness of the learning plan, and improves it when necessary
levels.5: Role models consistently seeking performance data with adaptability, and mentors others on reflective practice

levels.1: Demonstrates professional/ethical behavior in routine situations
levels.2: Demonstrates a pattern of professional/ethical behavior in routine situations and takes responsibility when there are lapses
levels.3: Demonstrates a pattern of professional/ethical behavior in complex or stressful situations
levels.4: Recognizes situations that may cause professional/ethical lapses in others and intervenes to prevent lapses in oneself and others
levels.5: Mentors others when their behavior fails to meet professional expectations

levels.1: Performs tasks and responsibilities with prompting
levels.2: Performs tasks and responsibilities in a timely manner with attention to detail in routine situations
levels.3: Performs tasks and responsibilities in a timely manner with attention to detail in complex or stressful situations
levels.4: Proactively implements strategies to ensure that the needs of patients, teams, and systems are met
levels.5: Creates strategies to enhance others’ ability to efficiently complete tasks and responsibilities

levels.2: Describes resources that are meant to promote well-being
levels.3: Recognizes status of well-being in oneself or peers and knows how to report concerns to appropriate personnel
levels.4: Develops a plan to improve well-being of oneself or peers, including use of institutional or external resources
levels.5: Creates institutional-level interventions that promote colleagues’ well-being

subcompetencies.name_zh: 以病人與家屬為中心之溝通

levels.1: Uses clear language and non-verbal behavior to demonstrate respect and establish rapport Recognizes common barriers to effective communication (e.g., language, disability)
levels.2: Establishes a therapeutic relationship with patients using active listening and clear language in straightforward encounters Recognizes complex barriers to effective communication (e.g., health literacy)
levels.3: Establishes a therapeutic relationship with patients’ and their families in challenging patient encounters Adjusts communication strategies based on identified barriers, incorporating patient and caregiver expectations and goals of care
levels.4: Independently uses shared decision making to make a personalized care plan Proactively improves communication by addressing barriers and biases
levels.5: Serves as a role model in establishing respectful, culturally sensitive, therapeutic relationships while mitigating communication barriers

levels.1: Uses language that is respectful and values all members of the health care team
levels.2: Communicates basic information and provides feedback to all health care team members
levels.3: Communicates complex information and provides difficult feedback to all health care team members
levels.4: Optimizes communication strategies using input from all team members to build consensus and resolve conflicts as needed
levels.5: Demonstrates leadership in promoting open and safe communication within and between teams

levels.1: Verifies and accurately records current and relevant information in the patient's chart
levels.2: Concisely documents diagnostic and therapeutic reasoning, accurately reflecting patient course for common conditions
levels.3: Concisely documents diagnostic and therapeutic reasoning, accurately reflecting patient course for complex conditions
levels.4: Concisely documents diagnostic and therapeutic reasoning, accurately reflecting patient course for all conditions while satisfying institutional billing needs and compliance
levels.5: Mentors others in in documenting diagnostic and therapeutic reasoning, accurately reflecting patient course Guides departmental or institutional documentation policy and procedures
